Entertaining so far after two episodes, and I am enjoying the series. I like the storyline and the characters. Kate does a good job, and I like Brenda Song is solid. Comedy is good, and you can expect some cheesy portions given the genre. Only part that gets me rolling my eyes is the general broad strokes of clear wokism. I'm all for calling out bad rich people, female empowerment, and non gay hate....it's just the way this show goes about brining that to the discussion is just odd and distasteful for me. I can tell how Mindy Khaling has put her fingers on it with all the trash lines where they leave no room for interpretation and just straight up say every plot line and thought. Plus the: "white men, misogynistic, rich man bad, billionaires are evil, girl boss cringe, token gay character, etc etc." Not as bad as Mindy Khaling's dumpster fire: "Velma", but I'll keep watching Running Point and give it more of a chance. But I'm sure I will continue to eye roll at the writing and script at times haha

Running Point" is a slam dunk! As a huge fan of sports dramas, I was blown away by this captivating series. The show masterfully weaves together the complexities of family dynamics, the cutthroat world of professional basketball, and the triumphs of female leadership.
Kate Hudson shines as the president of the basketball team, bringing her signature wit, charm, and determination to the role. Her character's sharp instincts and life experiences make her the perfect candidate to navigate the testosterone-fueled world of basketball.
The entire production team and creators deserve a standing ovation for their outstanding work. The writing is crisp, the acting is superb, and the direction is top-notch.
If you're looking for a compelling and entertaining watch, I highly recommend "Running Point". Trust me, you won't be disappointed!
